channel.title: Frontion News - Global Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: Mexico: US agents killed in crash not authorized for local operations
item.description: Two US federal agents, reportedly CIA, died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab. Mexican authorities say they were not permitted to operate in the country.

item.title: Trump cancels US envoys trip to Pakistan for Iran war talks
item.description: US President cancelled the delegation trip led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner after Iran said there were no plans for direct meeting. Peace prospects dim further.

item.title: Palestinians hold local elections in West Bank and Gaza
item.description: Local elections conducted in the occupied West Bank and one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups did not participate.

item.title: Afghanistan urges Afghans stuck in Qatar to return home
item.description: Taliban government calls on Afghans who helped US forces and are now stranded in Qatar to return to Afghanistan.

item.title: Venezuela prisoner release scheme nearing end, rights groups warn
item.description: More than 500 political prisoners remain jailed as amnesty program winds down amid criticism from human rights organizations.

item.title: US-Iran Peace Hopes Fade as Trump Cancels Negotiator Trip
item.description: President Trump canceled a planned trip by envoys Kushner and Witkoff to Pakistan for Iran ceasefire talks, citing inadequate Iranian proposals. Both sides show little willingness to soften terms as the war enters a stalemate phase.

item.title: Germany Suspects Russia Behind Signal Phishing Campaign Targeting Politicians
item.description: The German government believes Russia orchestrated phishing attacks via the Signal messaging app targeting high-profile politicians, diplomats, military officers, and journalists. At least 300 accounts were compromised in the cyber campaign.

item.title: Ukrainian Drone Strikes Apartment Building in Yekaterinburg
item.description: A Ukrainian drone hit a multi-story apartment building in Russia's fourth-largest city of Yekaterinburg, marking the first attack on the city since the conflict began. Regional authorities reported damage to residential infrastructure.

item.title: Colombia Bus Bombing Kills 13 Ahead of Presidential Election
item.description: At least 13 people were killed and 17 wounded in an explosives attack on a bus in western Colombia. Authorities blamed dissidents of the FARC guerrilla group for the violence amid escalating pre-election tensions.

item.title: Finland Joins Nordic Boycott Over Russian Athlete Decision
item.description: Finland announced it will not host international championships while World Aquatics allows Russian and Belarusian athletes to compete, joining Norway and Poland in protest against the governing body's policy.

item.title: Hezbollah accused of sabotaging Israel-Lebanon peace efforts amid ceasefire extension
item.description: Israeli PM Netanyahu said Hezbollah is trying to derail a potential historic peace agreement with Lebanon, after Israeli forces struck Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon in response to ceasefire violations.

item.title: Chinese experts detail how Iran detected and damaged US F-35 stealth jet
item.description: Chinese military analysts published a detailed breakdown of how Iranian air defenses identified and struck an American F-35 during operations, raising questions about stealth aircraft survivability.

item.title: Dutch Military Chief Warns Russia Could Attack NATO Within Year of Ukraine War End
item.description: Netherlands' defense chief stated Russia may be ready for conflict with NATO within one year after concluding operations in Ukraine, urging allied forces to accelerate rearmament and preparedness.

item.title: Thailand and Cambodia Sign Ceasefire with US Observers
item.description: Thailand and Cambodia signed a ceasefire agreement ending border clashes, with President Trump personally overseeing the negotiations in a rare diplomatic success for the administration.

item.title: Russian Defense Minister Visits North Korea
item.description: Russian Defense Minister Andrei Belousov traveled to North Korea for meetings with Kim Jong Un's military leadership, discussing continued military cooperation amid the Ukraine conflict.

item.title: CNN: White House Correspondents Dinner Shooting Coverage
item.description: Live updates on the incident where shots were fired outside the White House Correspondents' Dinner, prompting Secret Service to rush President Trump away from the venue.

item.title: Macron Reaffirms Efforts to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: French President Macron pledged continued diplomatic efforts to ensure freedom of navigation through the Strait of Hormuz, as TotalEnergies warned about global energy supply risks.

item.title: Egypt's Economic Outlook Trimmed Due to Iran War
item.description: Egypt's GDP growth forecast was reduced amid regional economic fallout from the US-Iran conflict, with Suez Canal transit revenues significantly impacted.

item.title: Greece and France Sign Major Defense Agreement
item.description: Greece and France finalized a comprehensive defense pact covering military equipment procurement, joint training, and mutual defense provisions, strengthening NATO's southeastern flank.

item.title: AP: White House Correspondents Dinner Live Coverage
item.description: Breaking news coverage of the security incident at the annual White House Correspondents' Dinner where gunfire was reported outside the venue.

item.title: BBC: Live Updates on White House Correspondents Dinner Shooting
item.description: Live coverage of the incident at the Washington Hilton where shots were fired as President Trump attended the annual press dinner.

item.title: BBC: Putin Faces Rising Discontent Over War
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is encountering growing domestic opposition to the Ukraine war, with approval ratings slipping amid economic pressures and military setbacks.

item.title: BBC: Seven Dead in Major Russian Attack on Ukraine
item.description: At least seven people were killed in a large-scale Russian missile and drone attack on Ukrainian cities, with Dnipro suffering the heaviest casualties.

item.title: BBC: Netanyahu Orders Vigorous Attacks on Hezbollah
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu directed the military to intensify strikes against Hezbollah in Lebanon, despite ongoing ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: BBC: Armed Groups Launch Coordinated Attacks Across Mali
item.description: Jihadist groups executed simultaneous assaults on multiple locations in Mali's center and north, marking the largest such attack in years according to witnesses.
